There is a condition that affects one in three women over the age of 40.  And this condition makes you gain belly fat without having to eat much.  It makes you feel sleepier after eating, and makes you feel hungrier two hours after you've finished lunch.  And she sometimes appears normal in her tests.  Many doctors fail to identify this, and I, as a doctor myself, can assure you that if you don't understand it, you'll remain stuck, no matter how hard you try after years of dieting.  I'm Dr. Rina Botelho, I'm a doctor, and in this video I'm going to explain the most subtle symptoms that hinder weight loss in women over 40, symptoms that practically no routine doctor investigates.  So sit down, because this conversation is going to be a game-changer for you.  Listen, in the next 11 minutes I'm going to give you three things. First, what is high insulin and why is it so silent?  The second thing I'm going to give you are the seven signs your body gives you years before this diagnosis shows up in your blood.  So, take note, because you'll only recognize yourself by these loyal symptoms if you have three of them.  And the third thing I'm going to tell you, and this is the part that will totally impact you , is that the test your doctor ordered is insufficient to detect this metabolic problem you have.  So, let's go.  Look, let's start with the basics, but explaining it properly so you really understand, because every time you eat something, especially a type of carbohydrate or any carbohydrate at all, your pancreas actually releases insulin.  And what is the function of insulin? Its function is simple: to transport the sugar from the blood into the cells so it can be converted into energy for you.  So, think of insulin as a mail carrier. You go, the mailman goes, picks up a package, this package of sugar, and takes it to deliver to the cell group.  But there's a problem.  After 40, a woman's cells begin to become deaf, and insulin, which acts as the messenger, can no longer deliver the sugar package as easily.  So, what does the pancreas do?  Send more mail carriers.  Send more mail carriers.  But the package number doesn't change.  So what happens? Your insulin levels increase significantly with the same amount of food you ate back when you were thinner. That's why you eat the same things today as you used to, but you gain more weight now.  Because when insulin levels rise too high, and your insulin remains high in the blood, and you've eaten the same thing repeatedly, you stimulate body fat.  And that's a silent problem, because as long as your pancreas is able to send enough messengers, it's able to produce more insulin, great, wonderful.  But there will come a time when your pancreas malfunctions and you'll develop diabetes.  So, when you take the test, your glucose levels are good, around 90 or 95, then suddenly it starts rising to 100 or 105, and then your doctor tells you: "Your test is great, everything's fine, everything's normal, but your insulin is already where? At 25, 30, 35, three or four times above the ideal, because perfect insulin should be below five. So you have good glucose levels, but your weight loss is stalled, your belly isn't going away." And then I ask you, is it your test that's wrong, or are they analyzing you incorrectly? Because insulin starts to increase 5, 10, 15 years before the glucose changes, before you become diabetic. During all those years, you have an enlarged belly, no energy, your libido drops, you continue eating normally, but your weight increases, and to the traditional doctor, everything is fine . It's like a car. You have a car with an oil leak. For a while, if you...  If you're putting oil in that car, it will run. But then comes the moment when that little light comes on in the car's dashboard. That's it , the engine is destroyed. So when that light comes on, the damage is done. When your glucose test actually changes , the damage is done. There's no way to reverse this diabetes condition. So high insulin gives you symptoms, it gives you signs. These signs are silent and appear over years. But these traditional doctors only look at numbers on lab paper, on your test results. So remember what I'm going to tell you. Insulin resistance is not a sentence, it's a diagnosis that has treatment, that has a cure. Now, let me show you the seven signs your body gives you. It's there in your body showing that your insulin is high. So, write them down, because honestly you probably have at least three of these signs. First sign, abdominal fat that doesn't respond to diet. You have a belly, it doesn't go away. Even dieting, even exercising, even eating little, but your waist doesn't change. You change your legs, you change your arms, you change your...  Back fat grows, but belly fat doesn't change. This is a sign of high insulin. High insulin prevents abdominal fat from being eliminated. And then you're left with a belly, even if you're exercising, dieting, or eating little. Sign two: sleepiness after eating. You eat and then lie down for about 30 minutes after lunch. You think this is normal, but it's not. This sneaky feeling after eating is because insulin has risen and gives you that lethargy. Third sign: hunger a few hours after eating. So you eat quickly and you 're hungry again. And you can't go too long without eating. Because if you start going too long without eating, you get the shakes. Because when the insulin spike happens after you eat, what does the insulin do? It lowers your glucose. When glucose drops, your brain craves food. Fourth sign: intense craving for sweets, especially in the late afternoon. That craving for sweets isn't gluttony. It's not a lack of discipline, it's not a lack of willpower, it's biochemistry. Because insulin has increased from riboglucose, what will your brain ask for? It raises glucose.  Quickly. And then comes the fifth sign, a physical sign: skin tags. You know those little warts that appear on the neck? They look like little skin tags, appearing on the neck, groin, armpits, and back. A classic sign of insulin resistance. Almost no doctor talks about this. They don't look at your body because they're used to just looking at exams and sending you home. And then comes the sixth sign: dark spots in the folds of your body. Neck, groin, armpits. Look here on your arm, if it's dark. That's a sign. This is called acanthosis nigricans. It's a spot that happens due to high insulin, not a sunspot. And then comes the seventh sign: difficulty sleeping. Especially if you're waking up between 2 and 4 in the morning, because there are peaks of cortisol and nighttime insulin that disrupt your sleep cycle. Sometimes you even sleep, but you wake up very tired, your skin becomes oilier. So grab your pen and paper, count how many signs you have, because if you have two or more of these...  If you have these signs, the chance of having insulin resistance is 70%. A woman with insulin resistance doesn't lack willpower. She has a cell that isn't receiving the proper signal. And then her body produces more insulin, and she gains more weight. And then there's a cruel problem. None of these seven signs appear in blood tests, in glucose levels, or in glycated hemoglobin. That's why they are silent. That's why the doctor has to be sensitive enough to look at your body, to look at your signs, because it's no use looking at numbers on lab paper, because that information isn't written there. And now the part that will impact you: the test that practically every doctor orders to investigate diabetes is the fasting glucose test. You fast for 8 hours, have your blood test, then 8 or 10 hours without eating, have your blood test, it measures your sugar, and this test only measures one thing: it measures when the sugar is circulating at that moment, but it doesn't measure insulin. And it 's precisely the insulin that is elevated and...  Most doctors don't order insulin tests. Think of it this way: insulin is like a cloth that wipes away dirt. As long as there's enough cloth, the dirt doesn't show. You don't see the dirt, but when the cloth starts to run out, you start to see the dirt because the cloth is gone. So, if you only measure your glucose, if your insulin is high, that insulin will keep your glucose low in your tests. Then the doctor says, "Wow, your glucose is low, everything's great with you." But when your insulin starts to fail, which is when your pancreas is failing, your glucose rises. So you've never had the right tests done? You 're doing routine tests, half-hearted routine exams, and then you're labeled as normal, a person who doesn't have problems, the famous "healthy chubby person," but that doesn't exist. The right tests to investigate insulin are three tests together. First, fasting insulin, the most important, perfect insulin below five. Above eight is already a problem, above 15, a big problem. And you'll gain weight without...  You need to eat a lot. And when you eat a little, you gain a lot of weight. The second test, the Rome-Rhino test, is a calculation based on insulin and glucose, and this test must be below 1.5. Above that, insulin resistance, and the higher the number, the worse. And the third test, glycated hemoglobin. This glycated hemoglobin gives the average of your glucose over the last 3 months since you last had a test; 5.6 or higher indicates pre-diabetes. These three tests together cost less than R$50 at the lab, take a day to get the results, and completely change the reading about your metabolic health. So, imagine with R$50, in 24 hours you have this diagnosis, and you've never had these three tests done together. Then you wait a week for a checkup, go to the doctor, you have a belly, you don't lose weight, and nobody asks you, nobody analyzes you this way. So you need to understand all this. High insulin is silent because its symptom isn't high blood sugar. It can take 10, 15 days to show symptoms.  It takes years for your blood sugar and glucose levels to rise, but your body has been giving you signals for years. For example, abdominal fat, yo-yo dieting, belly fat, hunger within hours of eating, strong cravings for sweets, small warts on your neck, darkened armpits, darkened areas in the folds of your body , difficulty waking up, and difficulty falling asleep again in the middle of the night . The right tests are three: insulin, HBr, and glycated hemoglobin. Fasting glucose alone won't diagnose insulin resistance, and it costs less than R$50 to have this analysis done. Your problem isn't just a hormone; it's a hormone plus your metabolism. Insulin is the hormone, but the damage it does to your metabolism certainly shows up in your belly, fatigue, uncontrolled hunger, and sleepless nights, and you're not addicted to carbohydrates. It 's not an addiction; it's a reward system. Once insulin destabilizes, your body will crave more carbohydrates, more sweets, and more.  Bread, more dough. I want to tell you something very important. In the next video, I'm going to show you how to reverse this, how to treat insulin resistance. 12 weeks without medication, you can treat this with a strategy that fits your routine, your budget, with small adjustments. So save this video, send it to anyone you know who can't lose belly fat, because this information about these three areas, this changes the game for most women over 40 who can't lose weight. Yeah.
